text.title: Laravel Queues Rules
text.summary: Idempotency, ID-over-model, retry/backoff configuration, failed() contract, and worker lifecycle rules.

rule.statement: Every job must be idempotent: assume at-least-once delivery and guard against duplicate execution.
rule.rationale: Laravel delivers jobs at-least-once; network issues, worker crashes, and TTL-expired WithoutOverlapping locks can cause the same job to run twice.

rule.statement: Pass model IDs not Eloquent model instances as job constructor arguments; re-fetch in handle().
rule.rationale: SerializesModels re-fetches on handle(); if the model is deleted, ModelNotFoundException fires. Passing IDs also keeps payload size small and avoids stale-model state.

rule.statement: Always check the processed/completed state at the start of handle() and return early if already done.
rule.rationale: Without an idempotency check, duplicate runs cause double-charges, duplicate emails, or corrupt state.

rule.statement: Declare $tries, $backoff, and $timeout explicitly on every production job class.
rule.rationale: Relying on defaults is fragile; defaults change between Laravel versions. Explicit values document the intent and prevent surprise behavior after upgrades.

rule.statement: Use WithoutOverlapping($resourceId) to prevent concurrent duplicate runs on the same resource.
rule.rationale: Without a lock, two workers may pick up the same job for the same order/user simultaneously, causing race conditions on shared state.

rule.statement: Set retryUntil() to bound the retry window; do not allow jobs to retry indefinitely.
rule.rationale: Without retryUntil(), a broken job retries until $tries is exhausted; for time-sensitive work (e.g., order confirmation), retrying after 24h is meaningless.

rule.statement: Implement failed(Throwable $exception) on every job that mutates state; update record status and log to the error tracker.
rule.rationale: Without failed(), terminal failures are silent; the order stays in a pending state with no indication of failure. failed() is the last safety net before dead-letter.

rule.statement: Set --max-time and --max-jobs on queue workers; restart workers after deploy via php artisan queue:restart.
rule.rationale: Long-running workers accumulate memory leaks; --max-time and --max-jobs bound growth. Restarting after deploy ensures new code runs on all workers.

rule.statement: Prune failed_jobs weekly via cron: php artisan queue:prune-failed --hours=168.
rule.rationale: failed_jobs grows unbounded; a large table causes queue:retry to slow down significantly as it scans the full table.

text.reference: Job skeleton with retry/backoff/timeout, WithoutOverlapping, idempotency check
systemd unit for production queue worker
